regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect southeast winds at 13 knots accompanied by drizzle and a 71% rain probability, which is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. Looking ahead to Port Everglades and Port Canaveral, drizzle with an even higher 77% rain probability and east-southeast winds at 10 knots will continue to affect embarkation and disembarkation activities over the next 24 to 48 hours. By day three, Miami and nearby ports will see a slight easing with south-southeast winds at 9 knots and a 55% rain probability, meaning drizzle may still affect outdoor plans but with reduced intensity. Cruise vessels should prepare for wet conditions and adjust schedules accordingly to ensure passenger safety and comfort.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today we have elevated winds out of the east-southeast at 20 knots impacting Nassau and nearby ports including Freeport and Bimini, which may cause moderate vessel motion and some minor delays in tender operations. The 36% rain probability with light drizzle in Nassau could affect shore excursions, so passengers should prepare for brief damp conditions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will remain steady at 18 knots ESE with partly cloudy skies and a lower 12% rain probability, benefiting ports such as Grand Cayman and Key West with smoother conditions and minimal rain impact. Beyond 48 hours, winds are expected to ease to 14 knots while skies become overcast with less than 10% rain probability, indicating improving visibility and calmer seas for ports including Ocho Rios and Santo Domingo. Cruise operations should anticipate elevated winds today but can expect gradual easing and improved weather conditions through the weekend.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, expect easterly winds sustained at 19 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 53% rain probability that may affect port operations and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix due to light drizzle. Vessel movements and embarkation in these ports could experience delays or slick conditions on gangways given the persistent drizzle and moderate winds. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will remain steady near 18 knots from the east with drizzle continuing and a 39% rain chance, which may affect Antigua and St. Kitts, requiring cautious planning for outdoor activities ashore. By day three, skies will clear to partly cloudy with an 11% rain probability and steady 18-knot easterly winds, improving conditions for smooth port operations in St. Maarten and surrounding islands. Keep in mind the consistent eastward wind flow which will maintain moderate sea states influencing vessel maneuverability throughout the period.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, an overcast sky with ESE winds at 11 knots and a 51% rain probability may affect port operations and shore excursions in Cozumel and nearby Costa Maya due to drizzle and reduced visibility. Mariners and passengers should remain prepared for intermittent wet conditions and moderate breezes throughout the region over the next three days.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated east winds at 25 knots in Aruba will keep sea conditions choppy, but with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, port operations and shore excursions in Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire should proceed without rain interruptions. Beyond 48 hours, winds will remain elevated near 21 knots eastward with overcast skies and minimal rain chances, suggesting continued breezy conditions across the Southern Caribbean that could influence outdoor activities but not halt cruise operations. Passengers and crew should remain mindful of steady trade winds impacting vessel maneuvering and shore excursion timing through this period.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, Barbados is experiencing sustained east winds at 21 knots with partly cloudy skies and a less than 10% chance of rain, which may cause some light drizzle but should not impact port operations or shore excursions. regions.central-mediterranean: Today, winds in Naples are light from the north at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation as well as shore excursions across nearby ports such as Civitavecchia and Livorno. Ketchikan and Skagway will likely see similar conditions with drizzle and moderate rain chances, so some outdoor activities could be impacted.
